note: source.url https://www.ul.com/services/ul-9540-energy-storage-systems returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://www.ul.com/services/energy-storage-system-testing-and-certification (verified live, official UL Solutions page covering UL 9540). Standard exists and is confirmed chemistry-agnostic (3rd ed, 2023, revised March 2025). mandatory_status (mandatory via NEC 706 / AHJs) and gap description are accurate. source.url has been corrected below.

source.url https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?UniqueKey=35690 resolves to UL 8800 (horticultural lighting, superseded) — completely wrong product. Correct URL for UL 1973 3rd edition is https://www.shopulstandards.com/ProductDetail.aspx?productId=UL1973_3_S_20220225. IMPORTANT content correction: the fragment marks Na-ion coverage '[UNVERIFIED]', but UL 1973 3rd edition (Feb 2022) explicitly defines sodium-ion cells and applies the same test requirements as lithium-ion (confirmed via UL standards analysis at un383.com and pv-magazine). The [UNVERIFIED] caveat in the fragment text is now resolvable — Na-ion IS explicitly covered under UL 1973:2022 Appendix E. The gap and mandatory_status remain substantively correct. source.url has been corrected below.

source.url https://www.ul.com/services/ul-9540a-test-method resolves and is official UL page. Standard exists and is de facto mandatory. Gap accurate. However, the fragment cites '[UNVERIFIED: confirm current edition]' alongside '6th edition, 2025': the 6th edition of UL 9540A was published March 13, 2026 (not 2025). The 5th edition addressed sodium-ion batteries explicitly; the 6th edition (2026) further strengthens large-scale fire testing methodology. The year '2025' in the [UNVERIFIED] tag is incorrect — should be 2026. Content otherwise accurate. Flagging uncertain rather than likely_error because the fragment already marks this unverified; no claim in the data body relies on the wrong year.

source.url https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-855-standard-development/855 resolves and is the official NFPA standard-development page. The standard exists; the fragment references the 2023 edition throughout. A 2026 edition of NFPA 855 was published by NFPA in September 2025 and is now the current edition. The fragment's repeated references to '2023 edition' are not wrong for US jurisdictions that have not yet adopted the 2026 cycle, but human review is needed to determine whether references should be updated to the 2026 edition. The 2026 edition added mandatory Hazard Mitigation Analysis and large-scale fire test requirements and expanded to non-lithium chemistries including sodium-ion. Gap and mandatory_status are substantively accurate for both editions.

source.url https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/02/10/2026-02575/hazardous-materials-harmonization-with-international-standards redirects in automated fetch (302 to unblock.federalregister.gov) but the document is confirmed real via govinfo.gov (https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/FR-2026-02-10/pdf/2026-02575.pdf) and PHMSA's own site. NPRM 2026-02575 is confirmed published Feb 10 2026; proposes to add UN 3551 and UN 3552 to 49 CFR 172.101 and directs shippers to follow 49 CFR 173.185 for Na-ion batteries. Comment period closed April 13 2026 confirmed. Final rule not yet published as of June 2026 confirmed. PHMSA enforcement discretion Dec 9 2024 confirmed. UN 38.3 mandatory requirement confirmed. All standards cited (UN 3551, UN 3552, UN 38.3, ICAO PI 976/977/978, IMDG 42-24) are real. mandatory_status and gap are accurate. The fragment's [UNVERIFIED] about final rule date is appropriate — no final rule has been published.

source.url https://www.ul.com/services/energy-storage-system-testing-and-certification resolves and is official UL Solutions page confirming UL 9540 and related standards. UL 9540 3rd ed (2023), UL 9540A (6th ed March 2026, 5th ed previously), UL 1741 SB (2021 3rd edition of UL 1741 with SB supplement), IEEE 1547-2018, and NEC Article 706 all confirmed real. NFPA 855 2026 edition is now published (Sep 2025) but fragment references 2023 — not a factual error since 2023 was current at time of authorship and many jurisdictions still adopt it; however the 2026 edition now exists. IRA FEOC restrictions mentioned as [UNVERIFIED] — appropriate caution. Gap on no confirmed public UL 9540 Na-ion listings as of mid-2026 is plausible; Natron Energy has UL 1973 listing but system-level UL 9540 for Na-ion BESS remains nascent. mandatory_status accurate.

source.url https://www.nfpa.org/codes-and-standards/nfpa-855-standard-development/855 resolves (official NFPA page). All standards cited (NFPA 855, NEC Article 706, UL 9540A, IEEE 1547-2018, UL 1741 SB, IFC 2024 Ch 12) are real. NFPA 855 2026 edition published Sep 2025 — fragment references 2023 edition; same note as naionus-fire-02 applies. Gap description and mandatory_status accurate. Na-ion-specific NFPA 855 energy thresholds are correctly noted as unestablished as of mid-2026. IRA FEOC restrictions marked [UNVERIFIED] appropriately. UFLPA/tariff treatment for Na-ion marked [UNVERIFIED] appropriately.
